entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Planning : Advance Formulas (Aggr...

entry.title: Re: To Park reverse document
entry.content: Is there any SAP standard workflow that can be use as reference?

entry.title: Re: Put a New ZTable on SM30
entry.content:
Hi,

  1. Check the Maintenance View (SE11): Make sure it has the key fields defined. If not, you won’t be able to manage it in SM30.
  2. Verify Table Maintenance Generator Configuration: Ensure the key fields are defined in the "Fields" tab and the maintenance type is correct.
  3. Check the Transaction in SE93: Make sure it's properly linked to the maintenance view and that you have the necessary permissions.
  4. Clear Cache: Try clearing the cache and then test again in SM30.

I hope I have helped you,
Best Regads,
David

entry.title: Re: Four Clean Core Use Cases for SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Business Technology Platform
entry.content: @Terry_Penner valuable content! Thanks for sharing!

entry.title: Re: Historical data migration from ECC to S/4HANA
entry.content: Ok Daniel, Thank you for highlighting. Could you suggest the right tag. I couldn't find a better one

entry.title: Re: How to get started with certification?
entry.content:
Hi @PatricKoglin 

  1. Please check our SAP Certification support site. there you find a wealth of resources including how-to guides, troubleshooting tips, and detailed FAQs about SAP Certification, all designed to help you quickly. If you can't find the answer here, create a case - our team will be happy to help you.

  2. Please access our learn by product site  SAP S/4HANA for enterprise portfolio and project management. and learn how to consolidate resources and centralize processes to keep the portfolio on track.

Does this help?
Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Active Budget Control in SAP Business ByDesign
entry.content:
Hi @Stef 

It it possible to have a budget maintained at a summary Cost Centre (CC), and this budget checked when the cost is posted posted to a child CC of that summary CC?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: How to use create condition contract when the conditi...
entry.content:
Hello,

If you need to use BAPI_CONDITION_CONTRACT_CREATE to create condition contracts and populate rates in a condition table which has PRODH2 as a field, you need to populate conditionkeydatain-prod_hier2. You need the following populated:

  • conditionkeydatain
  • conditionkeydatainx
  • conditionitemdatain
  • conditionitemdatainx

entry.title: Re: S/4 Public cloud - Service - create contact in servic...
entry.content:
Hello,

There is no feature planned to create a new contact directly in the Manage Service Quotations app. This would be an error. 

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: 🇵🇱 Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model and SAP generative AI
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event focuses on building AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model, SAP AI Core, and SAP Generative AI Hub.

During the event, you will learn about the fundamentals of the genAI, SAP AI Core, and SAP AI Launchpad. You will also learn how to use the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model to expose AI capabilities through your own OData service. While working through the exercises, you will become comfortable creating CDS-based service definitions and integrating them with the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and SAP AI Core Foundation Models.

This is an in-person event (not virtual) scheduled for Thursday, April 3rd, 2025, in Kraków, Poland 🇵🇱 from 10:00 to 16:00. The workshop will be held at the EPAM office and conducted in English.

Prerequisites: Bring your own laptop with a Chromium-based web browser, such as Chrome, Edge, or Vivaldi. Basic knowledge of JavaScript is beneficial, as you will be using the SAP Cloud SDK for AI with the JavaScript variant.

Please note that seating is limited to 25 attendees. Register for this Community Event using the RSVP functionality on this page.

The instructor will be Developer Advocate @Vitaliy-R.

For more information about SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with fellow attendees, visit the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Re: Success factors Compensation and Bonus configurations
entry.content:
@Sanjukta1 Benchmark fields cannot be made editable in Compensation worksheets. Regarding your lookup table something must be wrong in the formula or the design.

The way you have phrased your previous comments lets us think you are still "discovering" this module without proper certification, please provide proper credentials in a private message so we can help further otherwise we will not be replying to configuration help requests further.

entry.title: Re: CCM - WB2R_SC - Partial Settlement
entry.content: Hi @gvictor, When I tried to 'release to accounting' using tcode WZR2, its show a waring message 'document XX with document status workflow release required is blocked for follow-on document

entry.title: Re: Create new button with event SAP B1
entry.content:
Hi @RicardoTorresDev ,

There is a B1UP UF called HTTP Trigger. I've used its pre-defined functions, such as posting on an MS Teams chat, but never dealt with the option "Raw", which is where I believe you would create your HTTP call. However, this is handling outbound calls only, so I wouldn't know how you would deal with the response.

To be honest, I would rather go with a full functional solution like SDK + Service Layer, whereby you could create UI elements and use them to send/receive HTTP calls.

Best of luck!

BR,
Felipe

Hi @KuroKen 
Can you please share the ticket number?
Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Send PDF with XML file via email to your business partner

entry.title: Re: API LMS recordLearningEvents
entry.content: @Roberto_Di_Nota_All, the cause of the API failure could be the value of the "componentTypeID" field. It seems the assignment type ID is being added. It should be the item type ID (Learning Administration > References > Learning Activities > Item Types).

entry.title: Re: Can we add same subcurricula/item to multiple Parent...

entry.title: Re: Connect Postman with Cloud ALM to create Project usin...
entry.content:
Hi Sandeep,

You have mentioned the request "/solutionProcessFlows/{solutionProcessFlowId}/solutionProcessFlowDiagrams/bpmn". This is documented in the Solution Process Flow - Solution Process Flow Diagram section of the API Reference for the SAP Cloud ALM Custom Processes API on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub. Reading this documentation we find that the POST request requires you to include JSON matching the "SolutionProcessFlowDiagramCreateBpmn" schema. Here is an image that describes the schema, taken from the Schema page of the same API.

So this brings us to an important conclusion: there is no ability or concept of uploading the actual BPMN file when using the API. Rather, the "raw" BPMN data must be supplied to the API as plain text as JSON Body content in the Request. This is the only mechanism that the API permits to upload BPMN content to the SAP Cloud ALM tenant. 

If you have a BMPN file representing a process, you must first open it and copy the file contents (which should be plain text using an XML format). Paste the contents into a new text editor file. Then you must edit the pasted contents so that any special characters that can be interpreted by JSON are "escaped". E.G. If there are any double quote characters (") in the BPMN content, they must be replaced with an escaped double quote character (backslash and double quote, like this: \"). Then finally the edited file contents must be entered as the value of the property "bpmn" in the "SolutionProcessFlowDiagramCreateBpmn" schema. 

As an example, here is some dummy JSON matching the shape of the "SolutionProcessFlowDiagramCreateBpmn" schema. I haven't used real BPMN data, just placeholder data to illustrate the character escaping etc. I've also separated the lines of the BPMN contents for better readability, but they can all be kept on one line without any issues. 

    {
        "name": "My First Diagram",
        "bpmn":
            "<definitions xmlns=....
                 <process id=\"Process_1\">
                     ...
                 </process>
                 <bpmndi: BPMNDiagram id=\"BpmnDiagram_1\">
                     ...
                 </bpmndi:BPMNDiagram>
             </definitions>"
    }

The size of the BPMN content is limited to 10MB. If the BPMN file containing the BPMN text is larger than 10MB, then the content cannot be uploaded via the API. You will either need to reduce the size of the process diagram in another editor, or re-create the process manually in the SAP Cloud ALM apps.

The API request also requires you to provide a "solutionProcessFlowId" value as a parameter in the Request URL. You will need to execute a series (or chain) of various other GET queries from the Custom Processes API to find the correct ID value for this purpose. For example, you can work in this direction to get to identify the relevant "solutionProcessFlowId" value:

  1. Solution Process: GET /solutionProcesses => Equips you with a list of "solutionProcessId" values. Select the relevant one for the next request.
  2. Solution Process - Solution Process Flow : GET /solutionProcesses/{solutionProcessId}/solutionProcessFlows =>Equips you with a list of "solutionProcessFlowId" values. Select the relevant one for the next request.
  3. Solution Process Flow - Solution Process Flow Diagram: POST /solutionProcessFlows/{solutionProcessFlowId}solutionProcessFlowDiagrams/bpmn

Because of the various API chaining required to use this API, it can be quite complicated to use this API to create/update processes when compared to other SAP Cloud ALM APIs. You must really study the API reference and the Schema view to fully understand how to use this API. A certain amount of trial and error will allow you to build up the necessary knowledge and experience necessary to fully utilize this API to its full effect.

I hope that this information is useful to you. If you have specific issues, please elaborate on them and provide screen shots where necessary and I'll try to help further. 

Thanks and regards, 
John

entry.title: Re: Housekeeping LMS - Reports
entry.content: @Pablo_Asensio, the User Learning Plan Audit Report (CSV) can be used for the assignment history. For the login history, a custom report could be tried (KBA 3238442).

entry.title: Re: Create a Nested IF Else calculated key figure (KF) th...
entry.content: Hi @SoniaMarwah, if you need further assistance, can you please show me a screenshot of your calculation and a screenshot of your result in the IBP Planning View, and what is not working as expected. It will be easier this way.

entry.title: SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance, Integration Edition: Configuration Settings and Implementation
entry.content:
This blog addresses common questions about configuration settings and best practices for SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance, integration edition. It offers guidance on detecting and resolving configuration issues, adopting best practices, and utilizing helpful tips to enhance efficiency and ensure a seamless implementation.

SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance Overview

Access Analysis: Analyze access to identify potential risks, such as excessive or unauthorized access, and to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements by either refining user assignments or manage controls.

Role Design: Optimize process of defining, creating, and managing business roles within an organization to include cloud access and streamline governance processes.

Access Request: Formalized and optimized procedure for submitting, reviewing, and approving or denying access assignment requests. It ensures that only authorized and approved individuals can get provisioned to target applications and access what they are intended to, helping organizations maintain security and compliance. 

Privileged Access Management: Manage, control and monitor access to critical systems, data, and applications by users with elevated permissions by implementing strict controls and audit trails to prevent misuse or breaches.

 

Implementation Prerequisites for SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance in integration (Bridge) Mode with SAP Access Control

Connector Prerequisites:

  • In SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance:
    • Connect only one Access Control System per instance.
    • Follow KBA 3561030 (IAG-Bridge: Pre-requisites Jobs for Bridge Setup) for the sequence of jobs to be executed.
  • In SAP Access Control:
    • Ensure connector names in SAP Access Control match those in SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance.
    • Align Business Function Group and Connector Group names across both systems.
    • Include the cloud connector created in SAP Access Control within the connector group.

 

Rulesets and Mitigation Control Integration Prerequisites:

  • SAP Access Control mitigation approvers and monitors should match SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance groups.
    • Before synchronizing rules and mitigation controls from SAP Access Control to SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance, it is necessary to maintain the mapping of Access Control mitigation owners to SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance owner groups. This mapping should be updated in SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance under "Configuration" --> "Approver Mapping" whenever new owner and monitor groups are created.

 

Character Restrictions for Entities (Risk ID, Mitigation Control ID, Connector/Application):

Connectors and Connector Groups:

  • Connector: Ensure the name is limited to 20 characters as SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance only allows a maximum of 20 characters for the Application name.
  • Connector Group: Limit names to 10 characters, as the AC Connector Group in SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance restricts business function group names to this length.

Mitigation Control:

  • In SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance, controls should adhere to a 10-character limit. Maintain this length for controls integrated from other systems.

Risk:

  • SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance does not use rule IDs; therefore, risk identifiers should be limited to 8 characters.
  • The AC Target connector will not be displayed on the application tile in IAG.
  • Risks created in AC against a connector will be displayed in IAG against a Logical Group, named as connectorname_LG.

 

Best Practices:

  • Rule Set Synchronization: Exporting and importing rule sets directly from SAP Access Control to SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance is not supported. Instead, use the synchronization job, which includes the necessary mapping and conversion of the rule set format for IAG, rather than relying on manual uploads or downloads.
  • Mitigating Control Assignments: Mass mitigating control assignments for users or roles should only be conducted within SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance to ensure accurate access risk analysis results. Mass maintenance actions in Access Control may not synchronize correctly with IAG.
  • Workflow Management: Disable the mitigation control assignment approval workflow when the BRIDGE scenario is active, as the control assignments are managed directly in SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance.
  • Access Synchronization in Business Role Management: To ensure SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance accesses are available in SAP Access Control Business Role Management, utilize the ‘SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance Role Import’ option during repository synchronization jobs for cloud connectors. Subsequent updates to access attributes such as business process, criticality, and approvers must be maintained in SAP Access Control. Do not use the ‘Role Import’ utility from the NWBC launchpad for cloud access.

 

Troubleshooting Tips and Tricks 

When encountering issues categorized below, please proceed as follows:

  1. Job Logs Show Connectivity Errors in IAG Synchronization Jobs:
    • Cloud Connectivity Certificates: Certificates have a finite validity period. Check the cloud connectivity certificates of the subaccount when you encounter job log errors related to communication. Certificates should be updated before they expire. Configure alerts for expired certificates or when the cloud connector is down: Alerting | SAP Help Portal.
    • RFC User Configuration: Communication errors can also be due to issues with the RFC user configuration in the SAP BTP account destination of SAP Access Control. Ensure that RFC user accounts are not locked, expired, and that their passwords are updated in accordance with organizational policies. The RFC user must have proper authorizations as outlined in the SAP Help security guide.
  2. Missing Risk Descriptions and Levels in SAP Access Control:
    • Ensure any new risks and functions defined in SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance are synchronized to SAP Access Control via a repository synchronization job to your SAP Access Control system.
  3. Provisioning Status for Cloud Provisioning Events Not Updated in SAP Access Control Audit Log:
    • Verify Connectivity:
      • Use the IAGProvisionStatusUpdate service in BTP to run the "check connection" test. A successful test will result in a proper connection, as demonstrated below in the image.
      • If the connectivity test fails, check that the service IAG_PROVISION_STATUS_UPDATE_SRV is operational in SAP Access Control. Ensure the configuration settings match those highlighted in green below. If the settings are incorrect or the service is unavailable, contact your basis team to review the Cloud Connector settings. Install Connector | SAP Help Portal.
    • Service Check in SAP Gateway Client:
      • Ensure that the test is successfully completed in the SAP Gateway Client.
      • Verify that SSL is enabled for the IAG_PROVISION_STATUS_UPDATE_SRV service in SAP Access Control. The SAP Gateway Client should be enabled as outlined in the SAP Help document: Create Destinations for SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance Service | SAP Help Portal.
  4. Troubleshooting Repository Synchronization Failures in SAP Access Control:
    • Test Connections:
      • Use transaction SM59 to test connections for IAG_SOD_AUTH or BRIDGE_SOD_AUTH. If you encounter 201 or 500 errors during the SM59 Connection Test, this typically indicates an expired user password.
      • To resolve 201 or 500 errors, update the user password in the SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS). For guidance on configuring IAS user authentication, refer to the SAP Help document: Create Authorization Credentials | SAP Help Portal.
    • Firewall Configuration Issues:
      • If the connection test results in not reachable or NIECONN_REFUSED (-10) errors, these may be due to firewall configuration issues.
      • To address this, use the following to identify the IP addresses needed for the correct firewall port configuration settings: Regions and API Endpoints | SAP Help Portal.
  5. ‘System XXX Cannot be Connected’ Error in Access Control:
    • Refer to the knowledge base article for resolving connectivity issues related to the system XXX in the context of IAG - Error: System XXX cannot be connected (Bridge) | HCSM.
  6. HTTP Error 500 During Repository Synchronization Job:
    • For errors during repository synchronization for any cloud connector, consult the relevant KBA: Repository Sync throwing HTTP Error500 for cloud systems in IAG Bridge | HCSM.

 

 

If you have any further questions, please contact:

  • Shailaja Mutyala - shailaja.mutyala@sap.com
  • Senthil Chinnathambi - s.chinnathambi@sap.com

entry.title: Entity keys in an event handler in CAP using TypeScript
entry.content:
I'm converting a CAP project to TypeScript and the generated entity types work really well with CRUD event handlers, but for actions/functions the req.params object is generically typed, requiring extra effort to extract the entity keys.  It's odd since the req.data object is typed to include action parameters, so I would expect the key fields to be typed as well.

Below is my example code.  You can see I'm using a type predicate to inform TypeScript of the shape of req.params so it accepts req.params[0].KEYFIELD1, etc.

Is there a better way to do this?  I need the key fields since this is a mashup service that needs to include them in a call to an on-prem service.  req.subject has the key fields and values but I couldn't find a way to use it in an INSERT.  I was expecting that req.params would be a generic of type entity[].

 

import cds from '@sap/cds'
import { LocalEntity } from '#cds-models/LocalService'
import { OnPremHeaderSet } from '#cds-models/ZONPREM_SERVICE';

export class LocalService extends cds.ApplicationService {
    async init() {

        const OnPremService = await cds.connect.to('ZONPREM_SERVICE');

        this.on(LocalEntity.actions.approve, req => {

            // Have to use a type predicate since req.params (cds-types.Request_2.params) is generically typed as (string | object)[]
            // Could we use req.subject instead (which contains keys and values)?
            interface Key {
                KEYFIELD1: string,
                KEYFIELD2: string
            }

            const isKey = (param: string | object): param is Key => {
                return typeof param === 'object' && param !== null && 'KEYFIELD1' in param && 'KEYFIELD2' in param;
            }

            if (isKey(req.params[0])) {
                return OnPremService.run(INSERT.into(OnPremHeaderSet).entries({
                    KeyField1: req.params[0].KEYFIELD1,
                    KeyField2: req.params[0].KEYFIELD2,
                    Attribute: req.data.Attribute ?? ''
                }));
            } else {
                req.reject(400, 'Invalid request');
            }
        });

        return super.init()
    }
}

entry.title: Re: SAP EWM Exception handling
entry.content:
@Jamesvipbags 

You need to assign the exception code to the following step in node "Define exception code" (for product WT):

02 Desktop Product Warehouse Task

or in case of HU WT to following one:

01 Desktop HU Warehouse Task

entry.title: SAP service and asset manager more information about GIS
entry.content:
Dear Experts!

I have some doubts about SSAM Maps functionalities.

Question 1

In DEMO, when i create Functional location i can create selecting just one point the Functional location and appears the Functional Location Icon. But for equipment selecting just one point in the map, the equipment icon don't appear, just being possible to see when equipment has a geometry. Is it possible to visualize the equipment icon with one point in the map?

Question 2

In the scenario where i select a equipment in the map and i create an order in this equipment. In the map, i can visualize the geometry and when selected i can see the equipment and order created. Without i select the geometry, can i visualize some way the difference between a equipment with an order or a equipment without an order?

Question 3

Is it possible to enhance the SSAM map functionalities?

 

Question 4

Is there more functionalities or some difference for user visualize the map when using GIS or GEF?

 

Best regards,

 

Rafael Eij Umeda

SAP Service and Asset Manager

entry.title: Re: Class registration gone when attendance isnt marked a...
entry.content: @annafreihuber, if it's a VLS related class, the "Process VLS Attendance" automatic process can be used - this should be configured prior to the class start. Other than that, as it stands, he Learning instructor or admin would need to grant the class completion.

entry.title: Re: Couldn't drop a table or view from HDI artifacts in SAP CAP
entry.content: Hello, hope you're doing well. Would it be possible to comment here the rough steps you followed please? Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Streamlining Value Mapping Management in SAP CI: Exporting to CSV and Handling Large Datasets
entry.content:
Below code is the generic one can be used directly in cpi

 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;
import java.util.HashMap;
import java.util.ArrayList;

def Message processData(Message message) {
    def body = message.getBody(String.class);
    def xml = new XmlSlurper().parseText(body); //XmlSlurper does work in CPI, but the file creation will not.

    def groups = xml.group;

    def data = new HashMap();
    groups.each { group ->
        def entries = group.entry;

        if (entries.size() >= 2) {
            def sourceEntry = entries[0];
            def sourceAgency = sourceEntry.agency.text();
            def sourceSchema = sourceEntry.schema.text();
            def sourceValue = sourceEntry.value.text();

            for (int i = 1; i < entries.size(); i++) {
                def targetEntry = entries[i];
                def targetAgency = targetEntry.agency.text();
                def targetSchema = targetEntry.schema.text();
                def targetValue = targetEntry.value.text();

                if (!data.containsKey(targetSchema)) {
                    data.put(targetSchema, new ArrayList());
                }
                data.get(targetSchema).add([sourceValue, targetValue, targetAgency, targetSchema, sourceAgency, sourceSchema]);
            }
        }
    }

    def csvResults = new HashMap();

    data.each { schema, values ->
       
       def sourceAgencyValue = "";
        def sourceSchemaValue = "";
        def targetAgencyValue = "";
        def targetSchemaName = schema;
        def rowValues = new ArrayList();

        values.each { value ->
            rowValues.add(value[4] + "|" + value[5]);
            rowValues.add(value[2] + "|" + value[3]);
        }

        if (values.size() > 0) {
            sourceAgencyValue = values[0][4];
            sourceSchemaValue = values[0][5];
            targetAgencyValue = values[0][2];
        }

        def header = ",${sourceAgencyValue}|${sourceSchemaValue},${targetAgencyValue}|${targetSchemaName}";
        
        
        def csvContent = new StringBuilder(header + "\n");
        values.eachWithIndex { row, index ->
            csvContent.append("," + row[0] + "," + row[1] + "\n");
        }
        csvResults.put(schema.toLowerCase(), csvContent.toString());
    }




    message.setBody(csvResults);

    return message;
}

entry.title: Re: Auto-generating HANA ML CAP Artifacts from Python
entry.content:
@SrijaniChat 

With the design-time artifact generation, we basically generate the fit-procedure objects etc.

If the procedure is re-run (train the model further / train on current or updated data), a new model with the same algorithm and parameters is generated. So the artifacts haven't changed. What has changed is the input data for fit and the output model.

Thus here the model table would refer to the last trained model.

There are additional methods in hana_ml for model storage and versioning, which can be used.

Best regards,

Christoph

entry.title: Re: SAP build work zone, Custom Visualization of Tile is...
entry.content: I was able to achieve this - but the custom tile doesn't navigate to the main app. Any suggestions on this?

entry.title: Crystal Reports 2016 with varchar(max)
entry.content:
I am trying to use Crystal Reports 2016 with SQL Server and I am experiencing the issue with varchar(max) showing as String [255]. When I try to use the CR SQL Server Native Wire Protocol ODBC Driver 7.1.5 to connect to the database, none of the varchar(MAX) columns even show up. I am unable to map them because they are not present when using that connect. When I use an ODBC 18 or 17 driver to connect to the database however those columns are present but are the wrong data type. 

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/1256215

This is the exact issue that I'm facing and even when downloading the Supported Platforms Document it doesn't provide me with the correct driver I should be using. 

Has anyone else experienced this? Does anyone know of a solution? Would migrating to CR 2020 solve the issue?

entry.title: Re: Call for Speakers (SIT DFW) - spring 2025
entry.content: I sent another email today. did you get it?

entry.title: Re: Optimization of BAPI in mass upload for purchase orde...
entry.content:
Thank you for your response! I appreciate your suggestion regarding parallel processing and checking the SQL execution plans.

 

To better understand the parallel processing approach, I have the following questions:

 

What is the recommended way to implement parallel processing for a mass upload in SAP S/4HANA when using BAPIs? Would you suggest CALL FUNCTION IN BACKGROUND TASK, Asynchronous RFC (aRFC), or another method?

Since one of the BAPIs updates the purchase order history, would parallel processing cause any issues with data consistency? How can I ensure that updates do not conflict with each other?

If SQL performance is a concern, which specific tools or transactions should I use in SAP S/4HANA to analyze SQL execution plans for this scenario?

Thanks again for your help! Looking forward to your insights.

entry.title: Re: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Send PDF with XML file via email to your business partner
entry.content: It's working, but the customer logotype is in blank. Is there any limit or type of image that we can use? I made the download of the pdf using the API and the logo is there.

entry.title: Re: Develop Powerful Analytical Queries using ABAP Core Data Services (CDS)
entry.content:
Hello @MarkGreen ,

the learning journey about Developing Analytical Queries is really great content. I appreciate it.

I have one issue regarding the consumption of "transient views" though.

During the learning course from time to time you choose "Data Preview" and then a Fiori App opens.

When i choose "Data Preview" Eclipse shows error message with something like "Entity XYZ can´t be used here". However, when i build a cds view with "define view" or "define view entity" a data preview is possible in eclipse. I am then consuming the consumption view in the App "Query Browser" (multi dimensional web dynpro report).

I am using S/4 HANA 2023 FPS 2 OnPremise and i am wondering if "data preview in eclipse" and "Fiori App Query Browser" are working with "transient views".

In another blog you shared a video from Giselle Lin and right at the beginning she is showing and later using the Fiori App "Multidimensional Analysis" F5152A. Is this the App you are using in the learning course? This App is brand new 2502 for Cloud customers and not yet available for OnPrem customers.

So which option do i have to consume transient views in S/4 HANA 2023 OnPrem ? 

Thank you 😀

David

entry.title: Re: CRM Copy Badi Service contract to Order
entry.content: Hi Robert,

entry.title: Re: CRM Copy Badi Service contract to Order
entry.content: I am not able to open BAdI_CRM_COPY_CONTROL in se18.Is this the correct name..?

entry.title: Re: Guidance to configure PM Notification status for user...
entry.content:
Hello @Felix_A ,

You need to use the feature Lowest and highest fields in such a way that once a notification is approve then it can again be set to UNAPPROVE or PENDING status. You should use the numbers in these fields. It will require lot of testing.

Let me know if it solves the issue.

Regards,

Vaibhav

entry.title: Re: NF-e Technical Note 2018.005 - Technical Responsible
entry.content: Hi Rafaelvv, I have a question, how long did it take to register with SEFAZ PR? We have had a protocol open for over two weeks and it has not been approved and we have not been able to approve the NFe for Homologation. Is there anything we can do to speed this up?

entry.title: Re: Unintended line breaks when using code samples
entry.content: It will be fixed, one day, as commented at these two places: https://community.sap.com/t5/questions-about-sap-websites/edit-of-answer-with-inline-code-adds-empty-lines-around-the-inline-code/qaq-p/13583376, https://community.sap.com/t5/questions-about-sap-websites/blog-editor-does-strange-changes/qaq-p/13677917/comment-id/53619#M53619

entry.title: Re: The App "Manage Product Master data " does it shows c...
entry.content:
Hi @indrapats2800, 

You can see the Changed By and Changed On, if you add these two fields in the Manage Master Data view (they are not displayed by default).

However, it will not show the change log (previous values). Change log is only available  for Key Figures : 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_INTEGRATED_BUSINESS_PLANNING/feae3cea3cc549aaa9d9de7d363a83e6/8f6d3393dd0e480c8c5b1146b96eb2e6.html

entry.title: Re: APIs to Fetch Business Partner Blocked using ILM in S...
entry.content:
Hi VishalSingh,

In the SAP Help for the BP API, have you already tried the Data Controller and Handling?

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3c916ef10fc240c9afc594b346ffaf77/8e0c9c9c9adc42349b0959f0a71fa313.html?locale=en-US

thank you
Jerry

entry.title: Re: Develop Powerful Analytical Queries using ABAP Core Data Services (CDS)
entry.content:
Update:

Actually, based on the Fiori App Library the Fiori App "Multidimensional Analysis Reuse Component" (F5152A) is available for S/4 HANA OnPrem 2023 but the configuration sub section does not provide a business role or catalog so i am wondering if this App is really available and if this App i should use to consume transient views.

Beside that there are two more Apps Multidimensional Analysis (Key User) F7258 and Multidimensional Analysis (Developer) F7259 both are available only for cloud customers 2502 at the moment.

So my question still stands, how can i consume "transient views" on 2023 OnPrem?

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM and Servicenow Interation Error - 502 BAD_GATEWAY
entry.content:
Hi Arsul,

Are you using one of the "built-in" template mappings for ServiceNow? Or did you create your own mapping from scratch?

Based on the information you've provided, it sounds like the issue is related to how the mapping is configured. I'm not sure that the "source" and "sys_id" parameters should be included in the URL, my memory is that they should be in the JSON Body sent by SAP Cloud ALM to the ServiceNow instance.

What information do you see in the "Partner" section "Request Data" and "Response" data areas? Can you provide some example data? Feel free to censor/redact any Customer sensitive information. But please keep the existing JSON structure, I need to see what is leaving SAP Cloud ALM and coming back from ServiceNow.

Thanks and Regards,
John

entry.title: Re: SAP Clean Core Development :: Managed vs. Unmanaged RAP Business Objects - architectural differe
entry.content: I think draft handling is auto for both case.

entry.title: Internal Hire
entry.content:
Hello experts,

My client wants 2 different variants for their internal hire process variant. One variant for when the manager remains the same for the existing job and the new job the employee is transferring to, the other variant is for when the manager changes. I have created the process variants, however, I am unable to set up the business rule for selecting the process variant manager, where the manager remains the same. I don't see any option to compare the old manager with the new manager value. Has anyone come across this scenario before please?

Any suggestions will be much appreciated.

SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding 

Regards

Karishma

entry.title: Re: Mail in Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Simon_Lehnert ,

Have you already also setup the Email Sender Address, the SPF records and the DKIM?

DKIM is a request to cloud operations.

This is all outlined in 1LQ

Usually the SPF records and DKIM turn out to be the missing steps.

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: Re: Call for Speakers (SIT DFW) - spring 2025
entry.content: Yes @SergioG_TX , I am still interested. Please consider me for next session.

entry.title: Re: Internal Hire Skips Crossboarding the 2nd time On a Different Req from Recruiting
entry.content: We are seeing a similar behaviour. Did you manage to find a resolution for this?

entry.title: Re: New Resource-Related Intercompany Billing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi @varsha_chetanmanu, 

thank you so much for this great blog post. 
I am still figuring out, how I can access the information about the single time postings, which are flowing into the intercompany billing document. 
We are facing the issue that we cannot comprehend, how we can see the single postings, which are leading to the internal billing document? We are still using the old version with the debit memo request. Unfortunately we cannot find a solution on how to see all the single time postings. 
Is there a way to do this in the old and in the new version?

thanks.

entry.title: Re: Guidance to configure PM Notification status for user...
entry.content: thanks @Vaibhav

entry.title: Re: Guidance to configure PM Notification status for user...
entry.content: I have 3 statues

entry.title: SAPUI5 Tree Table not rendering correctly
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm hoping someone has run across this issue before. I have seen a ton of similar posts, but none of the solutions that I've tried has worked. I'm building this in BTP using Business Application Studio and connecting to our on-premise ECC system. 
I'm trying to build an SAPUI5 Tree Table with only 1 level to expand (possibly 2 in the future). The child nodes are not showing up under the parent nodes. Nodes 3 and 4 should be under the first node and node 5 should be under the 2nd node. 
The first two rows below should be parent nodes, I’ve added NodeID, ParentNodeID, HierlevelID and DrillState to the table just to show what values are coming through.

Appreciate your time and I'm open to any and all suggestions or other ways to do this!

 

The nodes do expand, but only a blank line is shown.

This is the CDS view that I'm using. 

This is my controller logic - only added to replace the 0 with ‘null’ for the ParentNodeID. The output is the same with ‘null’ and 0 as the value (If anyone has a better solution to get ParentNodeID set to 'null", please let me know).

 



 

My view.xml

<mvc:View controllerName="maintenanceplanner.controller.AssignedOrdersTree"
 xmlns="sap.ui.table"
    xmlns:m="sap.m"
    xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc"
    height="100%">
 
    <TreeTable
        id="idTreeTable"
        selectionMode="Single"
        enableColumnReordering="false"
     
        rows="{
            path : 'NodeDataModel>/',
            parameters : {
                treeAnnotationProperties : {
                    hierarchyLevelFor : 'NodeDataModel>HierLevel',
                    hierarchyNodeFor : 'NodeDataModel>NodeID',
                    hierarchyParentNodeFor : 'NodeDataModel>ParentNodeID',
                    hierarchyDrillStateFor : 'NodeDataModel>DrillState'
                }
            }
        }">  

        <columns>
            <Column id="idNodeDescCol">
                <m:Label id="idPersonnelText" text = "Personnel"/>
                <template>
                    <m:Text id="idNodeDescText" text="{NodeDataModel>NodeDescription}" wrapping="false" />
                </template>
            </Column>
           
            <Column id="idOperationDescCol" >
            <m:Label id="idOrderLabel" text = "Work Order"/>
                <template>
                    <m:Text id="idOrderText" text="{NodeDataModel>OrderNo}" wrapping="false" />
                </template>
            </Column>
           
            <Column id="idOperationActCol" >
            <m:Label id="idActLabel" text = "Activity"/>
                <template>
                    <m:Text id="idActText" text="{NodeDataModel>Activity}" wrapping="false" />
                </template>
            </Column>
           
            <Column id="idNodeID" >
            <m:Label id="idNodeIDLabel" text = "NodeID"/>
                <template>
                    <m:Text id="idNodeIDText" text="{NodeDataModel>NodeID}" wrapping="false" />
                </template>
            </Column>

            <Column id="idParentNodeID" >
            <m:Label id="idParentNodeIDLabel" text = "ParentNodeID"/>
                <template>
                    <m:Text id="idParentNodeIDText" text="{NodeDataModel>ParentNodeID}" wrapping="false" />
                </template>
            </Column>

            <Column id="idHierLevelID" >
               <m:Label id="idHierLevelLabel" text = "HierLevelID"/>
                <template>
                <m:Text id="HierLevelText" text="{NodeDataModel>HierLevel}" wrapping="false" />
                </template>
            </Column>

            <Column id="idDrillState" >
               <m:Label id="idDrillStateLabel" text = "DrillState"/>
                <template>
                <m:Text id="idDrillStateText" text="{NodeDataModel>DrillState}" wrapping="false" />
                </template>
            </Column>
   
         
            <Column id="idBasicStartDate" >
               <m:Label id="idBasicStartDateLabel" text = "Basic Start Date"/>
                <template>
                <m:Text id="idBasicStartDateText" text="{NodeDataModel>BasicStartDate}" wrapping="false" />
                </template>
            </Column>
           
        </columns>

    </TreeTable>

</mvc:View>

entry.title: Re: Guidance to configure PM Notification status for user...
entry.content: I have 3 statuses - Review = (Lowest =10, Highest =30), Approved = (Lowest =20, Highest =20), Approved = (Lowest =30, Highest =30).

entry.title: Any help please
Invoke Generative AI Hub Models using @sap-ai-sdk/ai-api library in BAS
entry.content:
Hello Experts

I am working with the @SAP-ai-sdk/ai-api library to connect to a configured Generative AI Hub and query the available models. However, I'm having trouble identifying the correct API or function within the SDK to use in order to retrieve the results.

 

 

 

async function main() {
    try {

        const { GenerativeAIHubClient } = await import('@sap-ai-sdk/ai-api'); // Dynamic import
        const client = new GenerativeAIHubClient({
            uaa: {
                url: process.env.GENAI_HUB_TOKEN_URL, // UAA URL from service key
                clientId: process.env.GENAI_HUB_CLIENT_ID, // Client ID from service key
                clientSecret: process.env.GENAI_HUB_CLIENT_SECRET, // Client secret from service key
            },
            baseUrl: process.env.GENAI_HUB_URL, // Base URL from service key or env variable.
        });

        // List Models
        const models = await client.listModels();
        console.log("Available Models:", models);

        // Invoke Model
        const invokeResponse = await GenerativeAIHubClient.invokeModel({
            modelId: 'sap/generative-text', // Model ID
            prompt: "Explain SAP BTP in simple terms."
        });
        console.log("AI Response:", invokeResponse.output);

    } catch (error) {
        console.error("Error:", error);
        if (error.response) {
            console.error("Response Data:", error.response.data);
        }
    }
}

main();

 

 

Getting this error as this api is not available in the sdk export list.

 

 

 

ERROR    TypeError: GenerativeAIHubClient is not a constructor

 

 

Could someone please guide me on the proper method/function/approach (Not using Python) to invoke and get the desired output from the Generative AI Hub?

entry.title: Enable Google Worksheets in S/4 Hana Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

We are implementing S/4 Hana Public Cloud for a client, who is not ready to use the MS products (Teams, Mail, Excel, Word, etc.,).

For any data migrations or transactional data updates using the Excel sheets in SAP S/4 wanted to be replaced with the Google Sheets. 

Also, when the client wants to extract/download some master data or transaction data from the system by default it does not give an option to download it to Google worksheets.

So we need a way to enable this feature in the SAP S/4 Hana Public Cloud for the Google space/Google worksheets enabled.

When I searched for this, I saw some external integrations needed with SAP Document Managed Services.

We also have the BTP and IS service in BTP used for this client. We can leverage these if required for the Google worksheets enablement in the client system SAP S/4 Hana Public Cloud.

Any directions to do this are much appreciated.

 

Thank you,

Hanu

entry.title: Re: How to get spare part list assigned to equipment BOM
entry.content:
hello @Badalsatyam ,

Here you go:

Step 1: get EQUNR (Equipment) from table EQUI and Pass EQUNR to table EQST. EQST will give you the link between Equipment and BoM.

Step 2: Pass the EQUNR, WERKS = Plant, STLAN = '4' and fetch the value of STLNR and STLAL

Step 3: Pass the values of STLNR, STLAL and STLTY = 'E' in table STAS and fetch the value of STLKN.

Step 4: Pass the values of STLTY = 'E', STLNR, STLKN in table STPO and fetch the value of IDNRK. This IDNRK is the BoM material assigned to the EQUNR (Equipment).

Regards,

Vaibhav Mishra

entry.title: Re: SAP Joule - Identity Provisioning - No assigned Roles displayed
entry.content: Hi Tobias, from Subaccount: Instances and Subscriptions, how did you get into Site Manager? Regards, George

entry.title: Re: How to know how the upgrade was done ?

Hello @Felix_A 

try this

20APPROVE2030
30UNAPPROVE2030

entry.title: Re: Did you know: SAP Support Log Assistant 2.0 Self Service Portal? & Trace errors for your IPS
entry.content: Please update the tool to also support the new "Central Store Provisioning" logs (which you can not download yet in Cloud Identity Service)

entry.title: Blog series on my CPI + Camel learning journey - Part2 - Camel in CPI
entry.content:
Link back to index: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/blog-series-on-my-cpi-camel-learning-journey/ba-p/14031053

Intro

First of all, Camel development to me is almost synonymous with Java development.
In a way, that it seems natural, that you have to be Java developer to do Camel properly. 
So keeping this in mind you might want to check proper official "Getting started guide" (and yes, camel app can be a real standalone Java application!)
 
But here we will first focus on abstractions Camel provides, and then try mapping it to CPI world (or the other way around) as we will do our best to keep away from any programming (including groovy) to follow the low-code paradigm.
 
Ideally, understanding of how "java-world-camel" really works under the hood could lead us (with help of our capic tool) to some insights of how we could achieve more in "low-code-world-cpi" (or maybe things we should avoid).

What do we have in Camel

So on this figure we see architecture overview
Not much to comment on, but that leaves us with following main abstractions:
  • CamelContext 
  • Route
  • Endpoint + Component
  • Processor
 
So far no Messages though!
Ok, lets try this one about CamelContext and see where we start having them maybe.
I strongly recommend thoroughly reading it through!
Here's the excerpt helping us to define entities we encountered earlier:
  1. The CamelContext is the runtime system, which holds together all the fundamental concepts of Apache Camel (Routes, Endpoints, Components, etc).
  2. Routes are a core abstraction for Camel. The simplest way to define a route is as a chain of Processors.
  3. To wire Processors and Endpoints together to form Routes, Camel defines a DSL.
  4. The Processor is a core Camel concept that represents a node capable of using, creating, or modifying an incoming Exchange.
 
Basically, it just so happened that CPI uses blueprint xml DSL to convert iflows into, thus giving us an option to play with all that stuff locally. But the "real one" is Java DSL of course.
 
And then there is really important section about Processors finally introducing Exchange and Message
There we read: "During routing, Exchanges flow from one processor to another; as such, you can think of a Route as a graph having specialized Processors as the nodes, and lines that connect the output of one Processor to the input of another."
 
And also: "At each Processor step, the output (Out) Message from the previous step is the input (In) Message of the next. In many cases, processors don’t set an Out Message, so in this case the In Message is reused."
 
Let's also have another figure from Red Hat Fuse guide also depicting Exchange flowing through the route.
There they define "Exchange object is a wrapper that encapsulates a received Message and stores its associated metadata (including the exchange properties). In addition, if the current Message is dispatched to a producer Endpoint, the Exchange provides a temporary slot to hold the reply (the Out Message)."
 
So now finally we see what's going on here: Exchange object is passed around, and Messages can be, well, exchanged by processors, so that next one receives what the previous returned (potentially NEW message).
 
To me it was confusing as in CPI they don't introduce us to this concept of "actual exchange" of messages (in plural), and this can mislead us to conceptualizing that both Exchange and Message are "singletons"
 
But here's some more info from this faq regarding In and Out Messages to clarify it finally:

  • The Out Message from each step is used as the In Message for the next step

  • if there is no Out Message then the In Message is used instead (!!!)

  • For the InOut MEP the Out from the last step in the route is returned to the producer. In case of InOnly the last Out is thrown away

Here InOut and InOnly MEP can be thought of as synchronous (sender waits for last Out message) and asynchronous communication. It is technically just a flag on Exchange though.
 
And finally, we might need something like this stolen from sap press blogs (who knows, maybe their book explains everything I am writing about in my blog series here) to illustrate Exchange to Messages relations.
 
So, key takeaways here are that Headers and Attachments are "attached" to a particular Message object (as of course Body is) that can be lost/exchanged, while Properties are on Exchange level.
Meaning, in theory, you can not rely on the fact that Headers and Attachments you add to your Message will "last" until the end of processing, while (exchange) Properties should.
Also don't forget that Out message (if processor returns it) becomes In one next step.
 

In short

So, to conclude this section let me try putting it all together in one sentence in a form of "The House That Jack Built":
  • Here's Message (having Body and Headers) we received through the Endpoint bound to some Component,
  • And transferred between chain of Processors (to be modified or exchanged) via and Exchange object (having it's own Properties)
  • Flowing through one or more Routes (defined in certain DSL) belonging to a certain CamelContext (sometimes separate)
  • Running in some OSGI runtime (thus "communicating" to other bundles) Apache built.
 
Basically, it means while there are some processors to process the exchange, camel route engine will pass it further down the graph.
The last processor to be called (not only within current route) is literally the end of the route processing.
 

What do we have in CPI

In CPI we have some other abstractions as iflows are modelled in BPMN notation.

So let's now try to take a look at CPI official documentation to find some hints at how it is mapped to Camel afterwards

 

Here's the quote:

"A graphical editor allows you, the integration developer, to model the message processing steps and specify in detail what happens to the message during processing.

In detail, you define the following aspects in an integration flow:

  • The senders and receivers of the message
  • How the senders and receivers are connected to the tenant (adapters)
  • The steps that define the message processing"
 
Ok.. not so informative, but at least this is something reminding us the Chain of Processors.
What if we now assume the following:
  • Adapters are camel Components (that are responsible for implementation of Endpoints logic)
  • Steps are camel Processors
  • Message is, well, camel Message (in most cases, In Message)
Next, we have another article in docs explaining "Camel Data Model"
 
Here we find that indeed "An integration flow is a BPMN (Business Process Model and Notation)-like model that allows you to specify how a message is to be processed on a tenant."
 
And also that "To interpret an integration flow model at runtime, it is transformed into an XML structure that is compatible with Apache Camel, an Open Source integration framework for Java that supports the mediation and routing of messages of any format."
 
What that really means is, our BPMN iflow becomes one or more camel routes within same camel context defined in blueprint xml dsl.
 
And yes, indeed they agree that we have Exchange object in place: "An Exchange can be seen as an abstraction of a message exchange process as it is executed by the Camel framework. An Exchange is identified uniquely by an Exchange ID. In the Properties area of the Exchange, additional data can be stored temporarily during message processing. This data is available for the runtime during the whole duration of the message exchange."
 

So what becomes what?

Let's now try to figure out how other elements of an iflow are mapped into camel model.
 
Well, it would be a good guess to think that Integration Process "shape" becomes a Route.
The same actually is correct for Local Integration Process.
But with Exception Subprocess it's not that easy - it actually becomes embedded in our route (we'll cover that later)
 
Another major difference would be that events (Start, End, Error, Escalation) also do NOT exist in Camel.
Though Start has at least semantics of from in camel world (as we connect our Sender adapter to it)
 
Other stuff would be more or less closely related to so-called EIPs
For example, Content Modifier would be a combination of setBody, setHeader, setProperty eips.
 
We're not aiming to cover it all here now (maybe later I'll do a separate post with some comparison table), but it is important to understand that on each step in iflow there is either a camel eip processor (eg router step becomes choice) or component/endpoint (eg <camel:toD uri="sap-map-pi..."/> for message mapping step).
// OK, sometimes like in case of HTTP receiver (ahc component, and I guess exactly because of Out Message existence) this becomes more complicated, so it is NOT a direct 1 to 1 mapping.
 
But what about receivers?
Well, again, no such thing in Camel, therefore we only have to (or toD) when we either define our Receiver as "final" step after "End Message" or when doing "Request Reply", or doing "Process Direct" and "Local Process" call - the only difference would be the Endpoint: direct (to route inside our Context), direct-vm (to route outisde our Context, but sap uses its own sap-processdirect) or some adapter like http or odata.
Anyway, in our route blueprint we will see something like <camel:to uri="..." />
 
This is VERY important to understand, and here's why.
As we said above, route is just a chain of processors we pass our exchange object to.
We only reach the end of that route when the last processor is called.
Now imagine we have an HTTP sender as we have in our simplest "Sender-initiated scenario with external data source and receiver"
 
It looks like this
HTTP adapter sets InOut MEP, which is not only synchronous in nature, but also means we shall respond with what the last message in chain is.
So, what would be return?
From what we see here it woud make sense to assume that we just return what our webshop odata call returns us, right? WRONG!
Why? Well, because we actually have something like at the end of this route:
<camel:to uri="sap-processdirect:foo?address=/custom/GenericReceiverDataStore"/>
So we kinda have something called "Message End", but does it stop camel from processing the exchange as usual? Of course, not!
Meaning, we actually will respond with what THIS guy will finish processing.
What I encourage you to do is to put Content Modifier where I have a red circle and set some random body.
And guess what? - you will see THAT body in your response from the Sender-Initiated scenario endpoint.
Again, this is exactly how Camel routing works, and this is exactly because of InOut (synchronous handling).
 

So what?

Well, I'd say, the biggest takeaway from this example is: in case you have InOut processing and don't change it to InOnly later (for example, placing messages into JMS queue, of which we have limited supply in CPI ironically), the whole processing is synchronous AND in-memory.
And that makes it oh-so-different from what you might read in this book where Message oriented architecture is Asynchronous (queue-oriented by nature).
This is where this BPMN low-code approach seems somewhat misleading to me in regards to EIP, ESB and all those enterprise level message oriented architectures: while it is possible to implement proper ESB on CPI, looks like it is NOT intended for really complex scenarions (eg cases like this with distributed transactions which camel supports while CPI does not even mention anywhere).
 

Moving on to Exceptions

As we said earlier, there is no such thing as Excepion Subprocess in camel, but there are onException clauses we can put to properly handle exceptions (who would expect that in java world)...
But then the question would be, how does End/Error/Escalation magic work?
Well, for those 3 examples from guidelines I made a new one almost identical to Sender-Initiated scenario we saw earlier.
It looks like this:
The main difference from this one is we don't use script step to "simulate exception" (why this even makes sense?..), we just instead rely on the fact that http adapter will throw one in case of 4xx/5xx answers.
In our case we will try reading Product by key, so it's trivial to trigger 404 here (which is also a rather realistic scenario, I'd say).
So, is there a way to see what does the camel route look like?
Yes, there is, and in our demo for this part I'll show it in action. Here are the screenshots of camel context outline feature we have in capic to quickly check routes and from/to uris we have.
So, here's how our End, Error, and Escalation events look like (respectively):

In all cases we see that "success path" goes to our Receiver, while inside onException things start looking weird.

Whats going on there?

Well, our outline only shows us part of the picture of course, but at least we can see that Escalation case is different as message is sent to sap-alert (which explains why you have some generic error in response).

How are End and Error cases are different though?

Well, with this another tool will gladly help us - git!

First picture is a diff between End and Error, while the second one - between Error and Escalation

Here we see <camel:handled> changed from true to false and also that sap-specific exchange property 

"SAP_MarkMessageAsFailed"=false is removed (meaning it is true by default)
Here we see that  <camel:handled> changed back from false to true and that <camel:to uri="sap-alert://escalation..."/> receiver showed up.

Basically, thats it: <camel:handled> just signals to Camel itself if route was able to handle the exception (and in the demo I'll show what happens when our local ftp sender case fails to do so), while some custom exchange property ("SAP_MarkMessageAsFailed") or any additional receiver ("sap-alert://escalation") does not make any difference  to Camel runtime.

And hence, this is how our local blueprint outline would look like:

In both cases we send message to our Generic Receiver, and the only difference is if we mark <camel:handled> as true or not.

Next Demo

Basically, we just modify our Exception Subprocess scenarion on CPI side, then sync it to local git and make local blueprints to compare them to each other.

It ended up to be a pretty long one though...

00:00 Intro and quick initial setup

03:28 Rezip bundles and test sample iflow (remote and local)

09:03 Sync iflow from CPI to git and make new local blueprint for End message case

21:07 Change iflow to Error event and sync and make local blueprint again

31:22 Change iflow to Escalation event and sync and make local blueprint again

40:13 (Unplanned) Revert iflow to End message and make a final conclusion regarding local blueprints equivalence

entry.title: Re: Unintended line breaks when using code samples
entry.content:
Thank you, @Sandra_Rossi.

I did search before posting, but didn't find the posts you linked. Good to know they're aware, even though the posts you linked are a year old at this point and the issue still persists.

entry.title: Re: Configuration Transport Center: New way to view, download and transport configurations
entry.content: @hariprasadgud. For the payment method issue we have logged Case 134043/2025 we have received the answer our requirement is not possible via CTC, see also this kba: 3552092 - MDF Object Records Not Fully Updated After Transport in Configuration Transport Center - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Setup Guide
entry.content:
Hi @nageshcaparthy  I hope you are doing well.


Our team has completed the Joule configuration for S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, however, when clicking the diamond icon, authentication is requested for the das-ias service, the das-ias service and S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition use the same IDP. Do you have any idea why this is happening?

If we authenticate with the same user who logged in to S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Joule works correctly.

Best regards,
Edgar.

entry.title: Re: Batch Validation/check during Sales Delivery
entry.content:
Hello, 

Please find below SAP Blogs that describes Batch Management in SAP Public Cloud from SD point of view:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/batch-determination-setup-scenario-in-s-4hana-cloud/ba-p/13498399#comment-645271

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/batch-management-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/ba-p/13558140

Also SAP Note:

https://me.sap.com/notes/33396

In these documents you will find information that batch expiriation date can be used as selection criterion during batch determination. 

Hope this documents will help you.

Regards, 

Jagoda

entry.title: Re: New Recruiting Offer Letter Template Rules - 1H 2024
entry.content:
Hi Calvin,

It's been a while since I've looked at this functionality, and not sure exactly how you are using it, but when I looked previously, the recommended approach was to set the rules to always true, and create a valid option for all scenarios, e.g.,

  • You are eligible for a 10% bonus
  • You are eligible for a 15% bonus
  • You are not eligible for a bonus

If the third bullet is not something you want to include in a letter, you could possibly add the dynamic token on the same line as the previous sentence in your letter but for the valid tokens you could include line spacing (<p>) so it pushes the text onto the line below, and for the other scenario just include a space with no line spacing. I haven't tested if this will work, just a theory.

Good luck.

entry.title: Re: Keep running Data Migration Job
entry.content: txs DDIC in a local system had been locked

entry.title: REDUCE not concatenating strings
entry.content:
Why does the following code with REDUCE not concatenate the table into the string 'ABC'?

 

DATA t TYPE TABLE OF kna1.
t = VALUE #(
  ( kunnr = 'A' )
  ( kunnr = 'B' )
  ( kunnr = 'C' )
).

DATA(log1) = REDUCE string( INIT x1 = '' FOR wa IN t NEXT x1 = x1 && wa-kunnr ).

WRITE: /, log1. " A

DATA log2 TYPE string.
LOOP AT t INTO DATA(wa2).
  log2 = log2 && wa2-kunnr.
ENDLOOP.

WRITE: /, log2. " ABC

 

 

 

The LOOP approach works just fine.

entry.title: Re: REDUCE not concatenating strings
entry.content:
OK, I discovered it is because x1 was implicitly char(1) and could contain only one character. Here is the corrected code:

DATA t TYPE TABLE OF kna1.
t = VALUE #(
  ( kunnr = 'A' )
  ( kunnr = 'B' )
  ( kunnr = 'C' )
).

DATA(log1) = REDUCE string( INIT x1 = CONV STRING( '' ) FOR wa IN t NEXT x1 = x1 && wa-kunnr ).

WRITE: /, log1. " ABC

entry.title: How to add more than 1 email notification recipient in a job in SM37
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm trying to add multiple email recipient inside the email notification section in a job in SM37. 

Is there a way to add more than 1 email recipient in the same job? Or there's a limitation for only 1 recipient?

Thanks

entry.title: PCo Agent status is shown as faulty.
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am using SAP PCO 15.5.4.16 and facing the issue of the agent instance not starting automatically and all its went fault & red while the Windows server is restarted occasionally. Those times we restart the interrupted agent instance manually.

The services startup type is automatic.

Can you guide to resolve automatically?

 

 

Thanks,

Mohan

entry.title: Re: Create Multiple HU for 1 Qty with UOM SET/EA in Inbou...
entry.content:
Hello, 

Here you will find SAP Documentation about HU and their packing instructions: 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/11fae0d93efe425cbead100510ab4b3c.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/e78cbf53f106b44ce10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

Also check Best Practises for this process:

https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-013/2408/SolP/4MM

For EWM purposes:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/9832125c23154a179bfa1784cdc9577a/6cfde482036c4e4589cb89ec228ff115.html

Perchaps you have set an automatic HU creation when you receive the product, changing into manual creation should be helpful. Also after receipt of product to your location you can try to split it into different HU. Creating suitable Packing instruction may also help.

Reagrds, 

Jagoda

entry.title: Re: Element info does not working in ADT Eclipse (2024-12)
entry.content:
Are you able to get the specific "window" for "ABAP Element info" in your IDE?
Via menu in ADT : Window => Show View => Other... => ABAP Element Info

Does that menu appear, and does that separate window work or is it also blank ...?

If your problem persists, can you consider re-installing / updating your ADT again for 2024-12 ...?

When all options are exhausted, I suggest you get in contact with some SAP-representatives such as "Julie Plummer" or "Thomas Fiedler" over this community ...

Hope this helps

Nic T.

entry.title: SolMan - incident status set to in process when RFC set to implemented.
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

When user set Normal Change to Imported in to production, the corresponding change request will set to implemented status.. which will again set the corresponding incident to In Process status.  This is by default.

But we don't want the incident set to 'In Process' in this scenario. The incident status should remain same like previous. No status change of incident is required.  Where I need to configure this in SPRO. 

 

Thanks,

Raghav

entry.title: Re: Cannot create BP getting run time error of ASSERTION...
entry.content:
Hello, 

Please find other Community Question with similar error:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/error-message-encountered-when-extending-suppliers-to-customers-using/qaq-p/12340294

SAP Note that refers to this problem: 

https://me.sap.com/notes/2915115/E

If you wont find solution to your problem with these dosuments, please provide more details regarding your case, because this error may occure with several different situations. 

Reagrds, 

Jagoda

entry.title: Re: Script for PPT upload in SAP Enable Now
entry.content:
Hi Romina,

Please send me an email to s.lipke@sap.com and I will send you a link to the script.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Fiori iFrame Preview Filename shows are $Value
entry.content:
Hi,

I have written a Fiori application that displays a pdf statement using an iFrame. When the pdf previews, the file name shows as $value? I am using the GET_STREAM method passing the header value but it still shows as $value. How do I get the file name to show?

Kind Regards

Deon

      ls_header-name = 'Content-Disposition'.
      ls_header-value = 'inline; filename="BuyingCardStatement.pdf";'.
      set_header( is_header = ls_header ).

      ls_stream-mime_type = 'application/pdf'.
      ls_stream-value = lv_pdf_xstring.
      copy_data_to_ref( EXPORTING is_data = ls_stream
                        CHANGING  cr_data = er_stream ).

entry.title: RE-FX Transaction Type derivation in contract early termination with direct posting
entry.content:
Hi Expert.

I have a scenario in RE-FX of early termination of a contract. My Valuation Rule has the "integration type" set as "direct balance sheet posting". I am checking the depreciation postings that the system creates automatically. I noticed that to my VA account has been assigned a transaction type "240" and to my P&L the "220". 

Can you please explain which is logic the system assign these two transaction types?

Best regards.

Michele

entry.title: Re: Can SAP PDMI alone move EBOM from Teamcenter to SAP
entry.content:
Hi Raghavan, unfortunately, there is nothing to confirm. ChatGPT did a lousy job! Here are my comments to the statements/questions below:

  • First of all, the strategic and current SAP Solution to integrate Teamcenter with SAP is called PLMSI and not PDMI anymore! And SAP PLMSI always need an Interface component for the specific 3rd party PLM solution. 
  • This means: NO, you cannot synchronize anything without T4ST. T4ST is the Teamcenter component you need in combination with SAP PLMSI.
  • T4ST is the specific version of T4EA to run together with PLMSI
  • SAP ECTR has really nothing to do with Teamcenter Integration. ECTR is the SAP PLM component to integrate Authoring tools (like NX or Catia) directly.

Hope this helps

entry.title: Re: Pop-up alert option in SuccessFactors
entry.content: Hi! You found something to fix your scenario?
